summary: whs4_nmp beacons installer path and host info to hardcoded Discord webhook on require
details: On module load, index.js unconditionally POSTs the absolute caller path (which typically embeds the installer's OS username via the home directory), Node.js version, and platform/architecture to a hardcoded Discord webhook at https://discord.com/api/webhooks/1523336123319849031/. The beacon fires immediately on require() with no user opt-in and no configuration. The package name (whs4_nmp), an internal default packageName of 'whs4_typo', and README text describing typo-triggered notifications indicate the package is positioned as a typosquat trap that harvests identifiers from anyone who mistakenly installs and loads it. Installer-side impact: leaks OS username, filesystem layout, Node version, and platform/arch to an attacker-controlled Discord channel on every require.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
